chunks.title: The Importance of Vitamin A
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the essential role of vitamin A for human health, particularly in relation to children with delayed puberty. Citing a study, they highlight how vitamin A and iron supplementation can be effective alternatives to hormonal therapy for such children.

chunks.title: Personal Experience with Delayed Puberty
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their personal experience of experiencing delayed puberty as a teenager and how a lack of proper nutrition, especially vitamin A and iron, may have contributed to this. They emphasize the potential benefits of including liver in diets to address these deficiencies.
chunks.transcript: here that is total the last piece of this equation nutritionally that i want to talk about is boron something that i spoke about a lot early on in my carnivore days when i wasn't eating any fruit i was just doing meat and organs and i thought where the heck are we supposed to get boron from on a strict carnivore diet it was really hard i think i found some study somewhere that suggested they were born in bones and so i was eating bone meal i tried supplementing with boron a little bit that did seem to decrease my shbg which went up on a ketogenic diet i'm going to show you guys my labs in a moment my shbg was essentially double on a ketogenic carnivore diet what it is now that i have added back in fruit and honey on an animal-based diet of organs meat fruit honey and raw dairy but

chunks.title: Exploring Boron in Diet
chunks.summary: The importance of boron in hormonal function is discussed, particularly regarding testosterone levels. The speaker reflects on their journey of finding boron sources while on a strict carnivore diet and how adding fruit has helped introduce this essential mineral back into their nutrition.
chunks.transcript: in that process it was important for me to understand what was causing the issue for me and one of the things i looked into was boron so a supplement of boron and i did feel increased libido probably because the shbg which was inappropriately elevated from long-term ketogenic diet strict carnivore was perhaps decreased by boron but as we know if you supplement with boron it tends to just drop shbg and then it rebounds back up boron is though apparently quite essential for proper hormonal function especially testosterone and interestingly you can get plenty of boron in your animal-based diet just by eating some fruit so i did a little research and found what appears to be a pretty accurate chart there are two studies cited here hunt at all 1991 anderson at all showing the fruits here apples apple juice applesauce bananas cherries grape juice orange peaches pears unfortunately there's no tropical fruit like papayas pineapples or mangoes but i eat a lot of bananas here in costa rica and um per kilogram of bananas i'm getting 3.72 gram milligrams of boron i don't think i eat two pounds of bananas per day but they're a good source of boron i'm also eating i'm probably eating five or six bananas a day which is something i've talked about now i'm eating some mangoes i will eat some papaya i will eat some pineapple and i do eat some honey and in fact honey is also on this list if you go down to the bottom here you can see that honey has 6.07 milligrams of boron per kilogram again not eating anywhere near a kilogram of honey per day but i am eating three or four tablespoons of honey per day so i think that on an animal-based diet i'm getting a really nicely adequate amount of boron the ideal is perhaps three to four milligrams per day avocado is also great in boron now if you look at that chart in detail they'll show you that boron is also high in things like vegetables or nuts and i would say that the boron which is a mineral is probably not very

chunks.title: Sources of Boron in an Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker provides a detailed overview of various fruits and honey as good sources of boron, while noting the limitations of obtaining boron from nuts and vegetables. They recommend specific quantities to aim for in daily intake to maintain adequate boron levels.

chunks.title: Nuts and Nutritional Bioavailability
chunks.summary: The speaker questions the bioavailability of boron found in nuts and vegetables due to potential digestive issues and phytates. They humorously suggest the inclusion of bull testicles as a unique source of nutrition, emphasizing a carnivore diet's benefits.
